response.justification: The candidate brings roughly 20 years of hands-on software engineering across web, embedded, data, and more recently AI-agent tooling, which satisfies the baseline tenure requirement. However, the role is a director-level individual contributor position centered on AI strategy, enterprise advisory, and recognized thought leadership in AI — none of which are evidenced in the resume. The AI exposure is recent and tooling-level (MCP generators, agentic workflow frameworks at a startup), not the depth of demonstrated AI products, platforms, or strategic customer advisory expected at this level. The candidate's strongest suit is broad full-stack craftsmanship and a founder-track open-source project, neither of which maps well to the core responsibilities of advising strategic enterprise customers or shaping cloud-AI product direction at scale.
strengths: ~20 years of continuous software engineering experience spanning embedded systems, high-throughput data infrastructure, full-stack product work, and recent AI-agent tooling
Genuine entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform, still active) and evidence of community teaching through workshops and conference talks
Recent hands-on work with MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq demonstrates current engagement with the AI tooling landscape
concerns: AI expertise is shallow and recent — no demonstrated AI/ML products at scale, no published research, no recognized standing in the AI field that would qualify for advising strategic enterprise customers
No evidence of director-level scope: the resume shows strong senior/staff IC work but no executive stakeholder engagement, large-team influence, or cross-organizational strategy responsibilities
Cloud depth is limited to AWS CDK and Kubernetes usage rather than cloud platform architecture or strategy, which the role requires alongside AI expertise
